jobDescription:
Java Developer

We are hiring a Java Developer to build scalable healthcare applications.

Requirements:
- 3+ years of experience with Java and Spring Boot
- Experience with microservices architecture and REST APIs
- Agile development methodologies
- Design and implement scalable, efficient, and maintainable Java applications
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and deliver new features
- Solid experience with object-oriented design, data structures, and algorithms

Nice to have: Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, JUnit, Mockito, PostgreSQL

jobBullets.title: Cerner — Software Engineer
bullets: Designed and implemented a Java microservice for claims processing using Spring Boot and RESTful APIs, improving data validation efficiency by 30%.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to define and deliver new features, ensuring alignment with healthcare compliance standards.
Integrated PostgreSQL with microservices to optimize query performance and reduce latency by 25%.
Automated testing with JUnit and Mockito to ensure robustness of API endpoints and business logic.

jobBullets.title: Optum — Software Developer
bullets: Developed Java services for healthcare data exchange, leveraging Spring Boot and RESTful APIs to streamline data integration.
Optimized PostgreSQL queries to enhance performance and reduce database response times by 20%.
Implemented automated testing frameworks using JUnit and Mockito to ensure code quality and reduce regression risks.
Collaborated with QA and product teams to deliver features through Agile sprints, maintaining alignment with business requirements.

jobBullets.title: Healthgrades — Junior Software Developer
bullets: Developed Java-based microservices for patient data management, using Spring Boot for rapid application development.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to design and implement RESTful APIs for healthcare data exchange.
Implemented unit tests using JUnit and Mockito to ensure code reliability and maintainability.
Supported Agile development practices and contributed to sprint planning and backlog refinement.
